sub: Technology block (d): Command Query Responsibility Segregation

title: What Is CQRS?
desc: Split the write side from the read side — and optimize each on its own

items: CQRS separates the responsibilities of commands (write operations) and queries (read operations) into two distinct models
The write model guards consistency and business rules
The read model is shaped purely for fast, convenient queries
Both are synchronized — often through events on a message bus (see Module 9)

body.alt: CQRS flow: write requests persist to the write DB, change data capture feeds a message bus, a transformation service builds the read store

title: CQRS in Air Traffic Control
desc: An ATC system processing airspace information — the textbook fit

items: Commands — change or create airspace state: issue a takeoff clearance, adjust flight zones
Queries — retrieve the current information about the airspace
The write model stores the current state of the airspace and guarantees all data stays consistent
The read model serves fast airspace queries without putting load on the write model
Controllers get instant situational awareness; clearance processing keeps its transactional integrity
✔ Scalability — read and write models scale independently; the read model is tuned for fast queries while the write model stays focused on consistency and safety
✔ Performance — air traffic controllers need instant airspace queries; a separate read model serves real-time data without slowing down write processing
✔ Reliability & security — only authorized users can change flight routes, while others get read-only access to flight information
✔ Flexibility — different technologies per side: e.g. a relational database for commands, a memory-optimized store for queries

title: CQRS Trade-offs
desc: The pattern is powerful — and it charges for it

items: ✘ Complexity — separating read and write models complicates the system: synchronization mechanisms become mandatory infrastructure
✘ Cost — multiple databases and a split codebase raise development and maintenance effort
✘ Eventual consistency — read data lags write data by design; brief inconsistencies are possible
In a critical environment like flight monitoring, that lag needs explicit countermeasures: staleness indicators, bounded lag monitoring, fallback to the write model for safety-relevant queries
Use CQRS where read and write loads genuinely diverge — not as a default architecture
